name: Modern History
description: Events and figures shaping the late 20th and early 21st centuries

questions.q: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall, signaling the end of the Cold War era?

source.notes: East Germany opened the border on November 9, 1989.

questions.q: Which U.S. president was assassinated in Dallas, Texas, on November 22, 1963?

source.notes: Shot while riding in a motorcade through Dealey Plaza.

questions.q: Nelson Mandela became the first democratically elected president of which country in 1994?

source.notes: Served one term, 1994–1999. Awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 1993.

questions.q: The Watergate scandal forced the resignation of which U.S. president in 1974?

source.notes: Only U.S. president to resign from office; August 9, 1974.

questions.q: In which year did the September 11 terrorist attacks occur?

source.notes: Coordinated attacks by al-Qaeda targeting the World Trade Center, Pentagon, and a fourth plane (Flight 93).

questions.q: The stock market crash that triggered the Great Depression happened in which year?

source.notes: 'Black Tuesday' was October 29, 1929.

questions.q: Steve Jobs was a co-founder and longtime CEO of which tech company?

source.notes: Co-founded Apple in 1976; died of pancreatic cancer in 2011.

questions.q: The Arab Spring is considered to have begun in late 2010 in which North African country?

source.notes: Sparked by the self-immolation of street vendor Mohamed Bouazizi in December 2010.

questions.q: Saddam Hussein ruled which Middle Eastern country from 1979 until 2003?

source.notes: Removed from power during the U.S.-led invasion in 2003; executed 2006.

questions.q: Ayatollah Khomeini founded the Islamic Republic of which country in 1979?

source.notes: Led the Iranian Revolution that toppled Shah Reza Pahlavi.

questions.q: The 1992 riots that followed the Rodney King verdict took place in which U.S. city?

source.notes: Six days of riots after four LAPD officers were acquitted of beating King.

questions.q: From which country did Pope John Paul II hail?

source.notes: Karol Wojtyła was the first non-Italian pope in 455 years; served 1978–2005.

questions.q: Margaret Thatcher served as Prime Minister of which country from 1979 to 1990?

source.notes: First woman to hold the office; known as the 'Iron Lady'.

questions.q: The Space Shuttle Challenger disaster occurred in which year?

source.notes: Broke apart 73 seconds into flight on January 28, 1986, killing all seven crew.

questions.q: Who succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO of Apple in 2011?

source.notes: Cook had been COO since 2007.

questions.q: James Cameron directed which 2009 blockbuster that became the highest-grossing film of all time?

source.notes: Grossed over $2.9 billion worldwide; retook the top spot in 2021 after a re-release.

questions.q: The 1982 Falklands War was fought between the United Kingdom and which country?

source.notes: 74-day conflict over the Falkland Islands; UK victory.

questions.q: The Channel Tunnel linking England and France opened in which year?

source.notes: Opened May 6, 1994; 50.45 km long.

questions.q: Pope Francis, elected in March 2013, was the first Pope from which continent?

source.notes: Jorge Mario Bergoglio from Buenos Aires, Argentina.

questions.q: The Large Hadron Collider — the world's most powerful particle accelerator — sits beneath the border of which two countries?

source.notes: Operated by CERN; 27 km ring near Geneva.

questions.q: In 2010, a mining accident trapped 33 men underground for 69 days in which country before their rescue?

source.notes: Copper mine collapse in the Atacama Desert; all 33 rescued via the 'Fénix 2' capsule.

questions.q: Martin Luther King Jr. delivered his 'I Have a Dream' speech in 1963 at which iconic location?

source.notes: Delivered August 28, 1963, during the March on Washington.
